senses.definition: an abbreviation for preoperative, relating to the period before a surgical operation
examples: The patient was scheduled for a preop assessment.
She attended the preop briefing with the medical team.

senses.definition: to open again or beforehand, depending on context; not commonly used in standard modern English
examples: They planned to preopen the store before the weekend rush.
The folder was preopen for the auditor to review.

senses.definition: the period before an opening; used mainly in planning contexts
examples: There is a preopening period for setup.
The team discussed preopening arrangements.

senses.definition: to perform tasks or steps before operating on something, especially in medical or technical contexts
examples: The team will preoperate on the specimen under sterile conditions.
Preoperate checks are required before the machine runs.

senses.definition: having undergone steps or procedures before operating; prepared beforehand
examples: The preoperated tissue was ready for analysis.
Preoperated equipment had already been calibrated.

senses.definition: performing tasks in advance of an operation; preparing something before use
examples: They were preoperating on the system to minimize downtime.
Preoperating checks were completed yesterday.

senses.definition: an operation or actions taken before the main operation
examples: The plan includes a preoperation to reduce risk.
Preoperation steps are documented in the protocol.

senses.definition: relating to the stage before operation (in psychology or planning); also used in mathematics and cognitive development
examples: The child shows preoperational thinking.
Preoperational planning is used in project design.

senses.definition: relating to the period before a surgical operation
examples: The patient underwent preoperative testing.
Preoperative instructions must be followed closely.

senses.definition: before a surgical operation
examples: The patient was reviewed preoperatively.
Preoperatively, the team explained the risks.

senses.definition: a device or person that operates before another; in medicine, a person or tool used before the main operation
examples: The surgeon relied on a preoperator to assist with the initial tasks.
Preoperator instruments were sterilized.

senses.definition: a part of certain fish anatomy, a bony plate near the gill cover
examples: The specimen’s preopercle was well preserved.
Researchers noted the color on the preopercle edge.

senses.definition: relating to the preopercle
examples: The preopercular bone structure was examined.
Preopercular scales appeared damaged.

senses.definition: the bony plate located near the gill cover of some fishes
examples: The preoperculum contributes to the protective gill cover.
Specimens showed variation in the preoperculum.

senses.definition: an opinion formed before considering new evidence; a prejudgment
examples: Her preopinion was formed before seeing all the data.
Avoid making a preopinion; wait for the full report.

senses.definition: having a fixed or strong opinion beforehand
examples: He remained preopinionated despite new evidence.
Preopinionated statements closed off productive discussion.

senses.definition: to oppose something before it is proposed or to prepare opposition in advance
examples: They began to preoppose the plan before it was tabled.
Activists preoppose changes they expect to come.

senses.definition: opposed in advance; having preexisting opposition
examples: The bill remained preopposed by several groups.
They presented a preopposed stance during the meeting.

senses.definition: opposing something in advance; engaging in opposition beforehand
examples: They were preopposing the motion in the committee.
Preopposing arguments were prepared for the debate.

senses.definition: opposition that exists before consideration or discussion
examples: There was a preopposition to the plan among stakeholders.
The proposal faced preopposition in the council.

senses.definition: to oppress beforehand or anticipate oppression; used very rarely
examples: The policy aims to preoppress dissenters before it is enacted.
This term is rarely used in modern English.

senses.definition: oppression anticipated or preexisting before a given event
examples: The speech addressed preoppression in the community.
Fear of preoppression affected turnout.

senses.definition: one who oppresses beforehand; a hypothetical or historical figure
examples: The text describes a preoppressor in the regime.
Scholars debate the existence of a preoppressor.

senses.definition: abbreviation for preoperative pain medications or preparations, used in medical notes; plural form
examples: The nurse reviewed the preops before surgery.
Preops included antibiotics and antiseptics.

senses.definition: relating to the area in the brain just above the optic chiasm or near the eye region; also descriptive in zoology
examples: The preoptic area of the brain is studied in sleep research.
Preoptic nerves were identified in the specimen.
